7438.history: Completed in September 1924. Displacement: 234 tons. HMRT Danube III (Lt. (retired) Richard Sullivan, MBE, RN) is mined and sunk near Sheerness on 13 October 1940.

7446.history: Completed in October 1937. Displacement: 487 tons. Bombed and sunk by German aircraft west of Tory island.

7447.history: Displacement: 282 tons. Wrecked on the Yorkshire coast on 2 March 1940. According to local fishermen, she was 'sitting pretty' and could easily have been pulled off on the next high tide. However the Captain refused the help of the fishermen and prefered instead to wait for an Admiralty tug. The result was that the tide turned Fairplay further onto the rocks and she broke her back before the tug arrived.

7458.history: Displacement: 197 tons. Became a survey vessel in 1943.

7460.history: Displacement: 700 tons. Became a barrage balloon vessel in August 1940. Reverted back to rescue tug in 1943. Returned to her owner in 1945.

Lenght: 45 m Diesel engines, 1240 HP Torpedoed and sunk by the German motor torpedo boat S 100 off Dungeness, England on 24 April 1944.The wreck lies in 32 meter of water in position 50º53'12"N, 01º00'22"E.

7479.history: The most powerful tug in the world at that time. Displacement: 793 tons. Armament: 1 12pdr AA gun, 2 MG AA. Machinery: 4200 HP

7480.history: The ship was at Batavia on 28 February 1942 and was ordered to escape to Colombo via Sunda Strait. However Evertsen (Lt.Cdr. Walburg Marius de Vries) was intercepted by the Japanese destroyers Murakumo and Shirakumo (both offsite links). Several hits caused a large fire, after which the commanding officer saw no other option than to beach his ship on the reef at Seboekoe Besar. All together 57 died during the sinking and internment and 111 were captured and repatriated though the Commanding officer Vries De dies whilst a Prisoner of war on the 25/03/1942. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Lambert Johan Goslings, RNN (18 Oct 1939 - 26 Mar 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Antonie Kroese, RNN (26 Mar 1940 - 30 Sep 1940)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Johannes Stephanus Bax, RNN (3 May 1941 - 1 Dec 1941)
luitenant ter zee 1e klasse (Lt.Cdr.) Walburg Marius de Vries, RNN (1 Dec 1941 - 1 Mar 1942)

7481.history: HrMs Piet Hein (Lt.Cdr. Jan Marie Lodewijk Ignatius Chömpff) was sunk by gunfire and torpedoes from the Japanese destroyers Asashio and Oshio (both offsite links) during the Battle of Badung Strait. 64 of her crew went down with the ship. See also this website (offsite link).
